title: Debugging and testing loop

publication.changeSummary: Topic-specific draft for Debugging and testing loop; requires human source, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review. canonicalExplanation.summary: A disciplined debug loop reproduces a failure, states a hypothesis, isolates a small case, observes relevant state, changes one likely cause, and reruns tests including a regression case.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: An off-by-one list failure is reproduced with a one-element case, the index is observed at the condition, the bound is corrected, and empty and multi-element regressions run. Your goal is to repair a flawed pseudocode routine and provide tests that expose the original defect. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Source-to-execution models; Examples and edge cases; Error categories and messages. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Operational model
sections.body: A disciplined debug loop reproduces a failure, states a hypothesis, isolates a small case, observes relevant state, changes one likely cause, and reruns tests including a regression case. The learner should use this model to predict an observable result rather than merely repeat terminology.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure point
sections.body: Debugger inspection can perturb timing, one passing example does not establish the hypothesis, and changing several causes at once destroys evidence about which change mattered. A correct explanation therefore includes the applicable assumptions and avoids this common mistake: Debugging means making plausible edits until the visible failure disappears, without preserving a failing test.

includes: Form a hypothesis, isolate a case, inspect state, change one cause and rerun tests.
Repair a flawed pseudocode routine and provide tests that expose the original defect.
excludes: Debugger inspection can perturb timing, one passing example does not establish the hypothesis, and changing several causes at once destroys evidence about which change mattered.

outcomes: Repair a flawed pseudocode routine and provide tests that expose the original defect.

examples.title: Debugging and testing loop: direct case
examples.situation: An off-by-one list failure is reproduced with a one-element case, the index is observed at the condition, the bound is corrected, and empty and multi-element regressions run.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the starting evidence: An off-by-one list failure is reproduced with a one-element case, the index is observed at the condition, the bound is corrected, and empty and multi-element regressions run.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Check the result against this correction: Use observations to test one hypothesis at a time and keep the smallest exposing case as a regression test after the cause is repaired.
examples.result: The case supports the declared outcome because Use observations to test one hypothesis at a time and keep the smallest exposing case as a regression test after the cause is repaired.

examples.title: Debugging and testing loop: boundary case
examples.situation: A total is wrong only after refunds; a focused test isolates one refund and a trace reveals the sign is added instead of subtracted.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed context: A total is wrong only after refunds; a focused test isolates one refund and a trace reveals the sign is added instead of subtracted.

Step 2 — Keep this boundary explicit: Debugger inspection can perturb timing, one passing example does not establish the hypothesis, and changing several causes at once destroys evidence about which change mattered.
Step 3 — Reject the documented misconception: Debugging means making plausible edits until the visible failure disappears, without preserving a failing test.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Repair a flawed pseudocode routine and provide tests that expose the original defect.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Debugging means making plausible edits until the visible failure disappears, without preserving a failing test.
misconceptions.correction: Use observations to test one hypothesis at a time and keep the smallest exposing case as a regression test after the cause is repaired.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using the direct and boundary scenarios, explain why this belief fails: Debugging means making plausible edits until the visible failure disappears, without preserving a failing test.

analogy.example: A repair bench can model Debugging and testing loop: identify the correct part, use a named tool, read the first reliable symptom, test one cause, and verify the repair.
usefulFor: Building a careful inspect, edit, run, diagnose, and verify workflow
limitations: Software tools have shell parsing, filesystem, runtime, and diagnostic rules that physical tools do not share.

Keep this boundary in view: Debugger inspection can perturb timing, one passing example does not establish the hypothesis, and changing several causes at once destroys evidence about which change mattered.
